name: Jerte Reservoir Loop from Plasencia
name_original: Embalse del Jerte y Vía Verde desde Plasencia – circular por el río Jerte
description: This difficult 38.7-mile touring cycling route offers diverse terrain and scenic views of the Jerte Valley and Plasencia.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Plasencia offers diverse landscapes, from the historic Vía de la Plata Greenway to the natural beauty of Monfragüe National Park and the Jerte Valley. The region features well-maintained paths through meadows, olive groves, and wooded pastureland, with routes often following river courses like the Jerte. Cyclists can explore dehesas of holm oaks and cork oaks, as well as the foothills of the Sierra de Béjar, part of the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. This varied terrain provides options for different skill levels, including both flat greenways and challenging climbs. Best touring cycling routes around Plasencia The most popular touring cycling route is Jerte Reservoir Loop from Plasencia, a 38.7 miles (62.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route offers challenging climbs and scenic views of the Jerte Valley. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Castillo de Galisteo from Plasencia – circular by the río Jerte, a moderate 26.8 miles (43.1 km) path. This route follows the Jerte River, passing by the historic Castillo de Galisteo. Local touring cyclists also love the City tour Plasencia – circular through the city, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through the urban landscape of Plasencia, often completed in about 48 minutes. Touring cycling around Plasencia is defined by historic greenways like the Vía de la Plata, the diverse ecosystems of Monfragüe National Park, and the river valleys of the Jerte and Tajo. The network offers options for easy rides, moderate explorations, and difficult ascents. The routes in Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Plasencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Plasencia?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes available around Plasencia, catering to various skill levels. These routes have been explored by more than 700 touring cyclists using komoot.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Plasencia offers 4 easy touring cycling routes. A great option for a relaxed ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105527822/city-tour-plasencia-circular-through-the-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour Plasencia – circular through the city&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.3 miles (11.8 km) long and takes about 48 minutes, allowing you to explore the urban landscape at a gentle pace.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Plasencia?
A: Cycling around Plasencia offers a diverse range of landscapes. You can explore historic greenways like the Vía de la Plata, which winds through meadows, olive groves, and wooded pastureland. Routes also take you through the rich biodiversity of Monfragüe National Park, with its mountains and dehesas of holm oaks and cork oaks, and the scenic Jerte Valley, known for its river gorges and rural paths. Many routes follow river courses like the Jerte and Tajo.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Plasencia are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105562013/castillo-de-galisteo-from-plasencia-circular-by-the-rio-jerte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Galisteo from Plasencia – circular by the río Jerte&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.8-mile (43.1 km) loop that follows the Jerte River, passing by the historic Castillo de Galisteo.

Q: What are some notable historical or natural attractions I can visit along the routes?
A: The region around Plasencia is rich in attractions. You can cycle past the unique Roman ruin of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/684512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco de Cáparra&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the medieval city of Plasencia itself, with its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6517887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lázaro Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo Boquerón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, often visible from various routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Plasencia?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for touring cycling in Plasencia. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and the spectacular cherry blossoms in the Jerte Valley. Autumn provides cooler weather and beautiful fall foliage. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Plasencia offers 23 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105470079/jerte-reservoir-loop-from-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte Reservoir Loop from Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 38.7-mile (62.3 km) trail with significant climbs and stunning views of the Jerte Valley, taking approximately 4 hours and 22 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I cycle through Monfragüe National Park?
A: Yes, cycling routes are available within and around Monfragüe National Park, allowing you to explore its wild nature and rich biodiversity. The park is known for its Mediterranean forests and as a sanctuary for birds of prey. Routes range in difficulty, offering opportunities to experience its unique landscapes of mountains and dehesas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Plasencia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from historic greenways to challenging mountain paths, and the beautiful natural scenery of the Jerte Valley and Monfragüe National Park.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options from Plasencia?
A: Plasencia is a key point on the Vía de la Plata Greenway, which is part of the longer Eurovelo EV1, the "Atlantic Coast Route." This historic route offers long-distance touring opportunities, following the old Plasencia-Astorga railway line through picturesque scenery. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo Berrocalillo Bridge – Casas del Monte Rest Area loop from Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km.

Q: Are there routes that showcase the Jerte Valley?
A: Absolutely. The Jerte Valley, located north of Plasencia, is a highlight for cyclists, especially during the cherry blossom season. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105470079/jerte-reservoir-loop-from-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte Reservoir Loop from Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; offer breathtaking views and wind through forests, river gorges, and rural paths, providing a dramatic backdrop of the Sierra de Gredos mountain range.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through historic towns or villages?
A: Yes, several routes allow you to experience the region's rich history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105562013/castillo-de-galisteo-from-plasencia-circular-by-the-rio-jerte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Galisteo from Plasencia – circular by the río Jerte&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you past the historic Castillo de Galisteo. The Vía de la Plata Greenway also features preserved railway elements and passes through charming villages, adding a historical dimension to your ride.
tips: Quiet road next to the Jerte river and the island park on the south side of the town of Plasencia. Shady park and great driveway to get out of town without traffic.
Quiet recreational bike lane next to the Jerte reservoir on the outskirts of Plasencia.
Spectacular views from this viewpoint over the Valle del Jerte. In this place you can also see the melancholic sculptures in memory of the forgotten, a 2008 work by the artist Francisco Cedenilla.
An incredible infrastructure project to convert an old disused railway line into a cycle path. This section passes through a short tunnel.

The 16th century medieval bridge crosses the Jerte River to communicate San Lázaro and the old town of Plasencia.

From this point you can enjoy the panoramic view of the river heading towards the mountain and getting lost among the vegetation.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde and this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Plasencia, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. The Vía Verde de Plasencia is easily accessible from the city, with the San Lázaro tunnel marking a key entry point.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Embalse del Jerte and Vía Verde circular route for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the overall route is rated as difficult, requiring very good fitness due to its 62.3 km distance and approximately 870 meters of elevation gain, it offers a mix of terrains. The Vía Verde sections, built on old railway lines, feature gentle gradients and are generally easy. However, the complete circular loop incorporates more challenging sections, including a short but steep climb to access the Vía Verde from the San Lázaro tunnel.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain profile. You'll cycle on paved sections (24.1 km), asphalt (21.5 km), compacted gravel (10.2 km), and some unpaved stretches (5.87 km). The river path immediately adjacent to the Jerte River within Plasencia is cemented, offering a smooth ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter diverse scenery and notable landmarks. Highlights include the Río Jerte Riverside Walk – Parque de la Isla, Plasencia, the serene Jerte Reservoir, and the Viewpoint of Memory, offering spectacular views of the Jerte Valley. You'll also get a View of Plasencia and the Río Jerte, and pass by the historic San Lázaro Bridge. The route also traverses the Protected Landscape of Valcorchero, characterized by cork oaks and granite formations.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Jerte Valley is known for its natural beauty, which changes with the seasons. Spring, particularly during the cherry blossom season, offers stunning views. Autumn provides pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde sections are generally accessible, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in more populated areas or near wildlife. Be mindful of other cyclists and pedestrians, and ensure your dog is comfortable with the varied terrain and distance.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Embalse del Jerte and Vía Verde from Plasencia circular route. It is a public trail accessible to all.

faqs.question: Are there any sections of the route suitable for families or less experienced cyclists?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. While the full circular route is challenging, the Vía Verde sections, particularly the 13 km river path along the Jerte within Plasencia, are very comfortable and considered ideal for families and less experienced cyclists due to their gentle gradients and well-maintained surfaces.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food, water, or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, some former railway stations along the Vía Verde have been restored and now offer tourist services, including restaurants, restrooms, and rest areas. Plasencia itself provides numerous options for food and drink before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What unique historical or engineering features can I see on the Vía Verde section?
faqs.answer: The Vía Verde section, part of the larger "Ruta de la Plata Greenway Nature Trail," preserves remnants of its railway history, such as old signal lights, signs, and switches. You'll also cross impressive engineering marvels like the 150-meter-long Puente el Berrocalillo with its seven arches, and the Gastón Bertier iron bridge.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Arriba, Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Abajo, and the Sendero de la Vera (Etapa 5). It also touches on the Camino Mozárabe por Trujillo and is part of the Eurovelo EV1 cycle tourism route, highlighting its international significance.
